title: Potentilla maculata gelida
taxonomy: Potentilla maculata gelida is a variety of the species Potentilla maculata, which belongs to the family Rosaceae. The genus Potentilla is a group of flowering plants commonly known as cinquefoils. Potentilla maculata gelida is a cold-hardy variety of the species, which is native to mountainous regions. It is classified as a deciduous shrub, with white or pink flowers and leaves that turn yellow in autumn. The classification of Potentilla maculata gelida at the species level is within the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the division Magnoliophyta.

intro: Potentilla maculata gelida, also known as the Himalayan cinquefoil, is a flowering plant species in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to the Himalayan region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Potentilla maculata gelida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
